Crisis Diplomacy

Yana Otlan

May 2020

Diplomatic Discourse by Topic

Hungarian Uprising

The red lines are the start and end of the crisis. Only texts that mention “Hungary” or “Budapest” included.

Suez Crisis

The red lines are the start and end of the crisis. Only texts that mention “Suez”, “Nasser” or “Egypt” included.

Cuban Missile Crisis

The red lines are the start and end of the crisis. Only texts that mention “Fidel”, “Castro” or “Cuba” included.

Berlin Crisis

The red lines are significant events from Katagiri and Min (2019):

  • 11/10/58: Khrushchev’s first ultimatum
  • 6/4/61: Vienna summit; Khrushchev’s secondultimatum
  • 8/13/61: Start of construction of the Berlin Wall
  • 10/22/61: Checkpoint Charlie standoff
  • 8/17/62: Killing of Peter Fechter at the Berlin Wall27
  • 9/25/62: Berlin Air Corridor incident
  • 10/10/63: 15-hour detainment of U.S. Army convoys
  • 11/4/63: Autobahn Tailgate Crisis

Only texts that mention “Berlin” and “Germany” included.
